message:
Implement AllocationCalculator with tiered formula (bd-1akfg)

- Add AllocationCalculator struct that computes agent allocations based on
  bead counts and tier thresholds from SwarmConfig
- Implement CalculateTier() to determine project tier (1, 2, or 3)
- Implement CalculateProjectAllocation() for single project allocation
- Implement CalculateAllocations() with priority sorting by bead count
- Implement CalculateTotals() to sum agents across all allocations
- Implement GenerateSwarmPlan() to create complete SwarmPlan with sessions
- Add session distribution logic that distributes panes across sessions
- Add helper functions: ProjectBeadCountFromPath, generateSessionName
- Add comprehensive test suite (16 new tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-19jd","title":"Task: Generate AGENTS.md from template","description":"## Purpose
Generate a project-specific AGENTS.md file with language-specific rules and conventions.

## Scope
- Detect project type (Go, Python, Node, Rust, Other) from existing files
- Generate AGENTS.md with base rules plus language-specific additions
- Include flywheel tool instructions (br, bv, Agent Mail, cass, cm)
- Do not overwrite existing AGENTS.md

## Detection Logic
- Go: go.mod exists → Go rules
- Python: pyproject.toml or requirements.txt → Python rules
- Node: package.json → Node rules
- Rust: Cargo.toml → Rust rules
- Default: Generic rules

## Language-Specific Rules
Go: go test, gofmt, go build patterns
Python: pytest, black, mypy patterns
Node: npm/yarn, eslint patterns
Rust: cargo test, cargo fmt, clippy patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- AGENTS.md created with appropriate language rules
- Flywheel integration instructions included
- File not overwritten if it exists (with --force override)
- Language detection works correctly

## Technical Notes
Use template/text with embedded templates
Check for file existence before writing","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:29:14.077650839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T07:53:17.978654391Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T07:53:17.978600539Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19jd","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a6c4","title":"Task: E2E robot-format selection (json/toon/auto)","description":"## Overview
End‑to‑end tests validating robot format selection and output stability for JSON/TOON.

## Scenarios
1. `--robot-format=json` produces canonical JSON (baseline)
2. `--robot-format=toon` emits TOON when encoder available
3. `--robot-format=auto` chooses TOON when available, JSON otherwise
4. Error path: TOON requested without encoder returns `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` or clear hint

## Logging (must be detailed)
Use `log.Printf` with structured prefixes:
- `[E2E-ROBOT-FORMAT] format=%s cmd=%s bytes=%d`
- `[E2E-ROBOT-FORMAT] fallback=%t reason=%s`
- `[E2E-ROBOT-FORMAT] error_code=%s hint=%s`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover at least `--robot-status` and `--robot-assign` outputs
- Output parsable by robot clients in both formats
- Stable snapshots (or golden assertions) for JSON payloads","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:24:54.921863332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:58:20.592689616Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:58:20.592109343Z","close_reason":"Added E2E robot-format selection tests for status/assign with JSON/TOON/auto coverage and logging","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a6c4","depends_on_id":"bd-1fmug","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:25:10.160391813Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1a6c4","depends_on_id":"bd-2dovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:49.312686531Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1a6c4","depends_on_id":"bd-2q3cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:25:16.836383841Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1a6c4","depends_on_id":"bd-3aejn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:42:13.824533080Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1a6c4","depends_on_id":"bd-4xgr6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:25:22.760047508Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1aae","title":"Task: Add ntm spawn --template flag","description":"## Purpose
Integrate template system with spawn command for one-command workflow initialization.

## Scope
- Add --template flag to spawn command
- Load template and apply all settings before spawn
- Support template argument overrides (--template=refactor --agents=5)

## Acceptance Criteria
- ntm spawn --template=<name> loads and applies template
- Command-line flags override template values
- Template not found produces helpful error
- Works with both local and user templates

## Technical Notes
- Template application happens before agent spawn loop
- Consider dry-run mode to preview template effects","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1alai","title":"Unit Tests: Robot Diagnose","description":"Unit tests for Robot Diagnose command.

## Test Coverage

### Health Classification Tests
- Test healthy detection (recent output, running process)
- Test rate_limited detection (pattern match)
- Test unresponsive detection (old output, running)
- Test crashed detection (no process)
- Test unknown edge cases
- Test edge case: agent in ultrathink (long thinking, not stuck)

### Recommendation Tests
- Test each status maps to correct action
- Test fix_command generation with correct session/pane
- Test reset_time parsing from various formats
- Test auto_fixable assignment logic
- Test recommendation ordering (priority)

### --fix Tests
- Test only auto_fixable items attempted
- Test sequential execution order
- Test partial failure handling (some fixes succeed, some fail)
- Test skip rate_limited items
- Test --fix with empty auto_fixable list

### Summary Tests
- Test pane counting accuracy
- Test overall_health classification thresholds
- Test panes grouping correctness
- Test --brief output format

### --pane Flag Tests
- Test single pane filtering
- Test non-existent pane error
- Test control pane (1) handling
- Test output format with filter

## Mocking Strategy

Mock status enrichment data to simulate:
- Various process states
- Rate limit patterns
- Different output freshness levels
- Mixed health scenarios

## Logging Requirements

All tests must include detailed logging:
- Log test scenario at start
- Log mock data configuration
- Log expected vs actual on each assertion
- Log full diagnose response for debugging
- Use structured JSON logging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:09.444366395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:32:07.028417016Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:32:07.028360339Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive unit tests in diagnose_test.go with 25 test cases covering: TestDetermineOverallHealth (13 cases), TestBuildRateLimitRecommendation (3 cases), TestDiagnoseRecommendation_JSONStructure, TestDiagnoseOutput_JSONEnvelope, TestDiagnoseOutput_EmptySlicesNotNil, TestDiagnoseSummary_TotalPanes, TestDiagnoseBriefOutput_JSONStructure, TestDiagnoseOptions, TestRecommendationActions (4 cases), and TestOverallHealthThresholds (3 cases). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-1pt6v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:39:28.774371521Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-26y68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:31.490695307Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:09.473784786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-3u6dg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:44.038995181Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-fzhuh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:39.711210482Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-kk81t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:35.439816788Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ay2","title":"Use lipgloss.Width() for all styled string width calculations","description":"## Problem
Width calculations using len() or utf8.RuneCountInString() on styled strings include ANSI escape codes, causing misalignment.

## Code Locations to Audit
- internal/cli/table.go - Table rendering with runeWidth(), stripANSI()
- internal/tui/layout/layout.go - TruncateRunes() function
- internal/tui/styles/styles.go - Gradient/shimmer calculations
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go - Various width calculations
- internal/tui/components/help.go - Already correct (uses lipgloss.Width)

## Current Issues
1. **table.go:runeWidth()** - Custom function may not handle ANSI correctly
2. **table.go:stripANSI()** - Stripping then measuring is inefficient vs lipgloss.Width
3. **TruncateRunes()** - May truncate mid-ANSI sequence

## Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
// BEFORE: Various approaches
width := len(styledText)                    // Includes ANSI bytes
width := utf8.RuneCountInString(styledText) // Includes ANSI runes
width := runeWidth(stripANSI(text))         // Extra allocation + regex

// AFTER: Consistent approach
width := lipgloss.Width(styledText)         // Visual width only

// For truncation
func TruncateSmart(s string, maxWidth int) string {
    if lipgloss.Width(s) <= maxWidth {
        return s
    }
    // Use ansi-aware truncation
    return truncate.StringWithTail(s, uint(maxWidth), "…")
}
\`\`\`

## Files to Modify

### internal/cli/table.go
Replace runeWidth/stripANSI with lipgloss.Width:
\`\`\`go
// BEFORE
func (t *TableWriter) calculateColumnWidths() {
    for _, row := range t.rows {
        for i, cell := range row {
            w := runeWidth(stripANSI(cell))
            // ...
        }
    }
}

// AFTER
func (t *TableWriter) calculateColumnWidths() {
    for _, row := range t.rows {
        for i, cell := range row {
            w := lipgloss.Width(cell)
            // ...
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### internal/tui/layout/layout.go
\`\`\`go
// BEFORE
func TruncateRunes(s string, max int) string {
    runes := []rune(s)
    if len(runes) > max {
        return string(runes[:max-1]) + "…"
    }
    return s
}

// AFTER
func TruncateToWidth(s string, maxWidth int) string {
    if lipgloss.Width(s) <= maxWidth {
        return s
    }
    return truncate.StringWithTail(s, uint(maxWidth), "…")
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
Add to go.mod if not present:
\`\`\`
github.com/muesli/truncate
github.com/mattn/go-runewidth (for non-lipgloss contexts)
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All width calculations use lipgloss.Width() or equivalent
- [ ] Tables with styled cells align correctly
- [ ] Truncation doesn't break ANSI sequences
- [ ] Emoji and CJK characters measured correctly

## Unit Tests
1. TestWidth_StyledString - lipgloss.Width gives visual width
2. TestWidth_Emoji - 🎉 counts as 2 cells
3. TestWidth_CJK - 日本語 counts as 6 cells  
4. TestTruncate_PreservesANSI - truncated string still valid ANSI
5. TestTable_StyledCellAlignment - columns align with styled content

## Verification
\`\`\`go
func TestWidth_Examples(t *testing.T) {
    cases := []struct {
        input string
        want  int
    }{
        {"hello", 5},
        {lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Render("hello"), 5},
        {"🎉", 2},
        {"日本語", 6},
        {"\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m", 3},
    }
    for _, tc := range cases {
        got := lipgloss.Width(tc.input)
        if got != tc.want {
            t.Errorf("Width(%q) = %d, want %d", tc.input, got, tc.want)
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log width calculations with input length vs visual width
- WARN: Log when width calculation fallback used","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:11:43.092942693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:55:06.350443242Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T08:55:06.350359103Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: table.go runeWidth() uses lipgloss.Width(), styles.go uses lipgloss.Width() for all visual width calculations, layout.go has TruncateWidth() using lipgloss.Width(). Tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ay2","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:25.813556494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1b9f","title":"Unit Tests: Round-robin distribution algorithm","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for the round-robin distribution algorithm.

## Test Cases

### Basic Distribution
```go
func TestRoundRobinDistribution(t *testing.T) {
    agents := []string{"cc_1", "cc_2", "cod_1"}
    beads := []string{"b1", "b2", "b3", "b4", "b5", "b6"}

    result := RoundRobin(agents, beads)

    // Expected: cc_1=[b1,b4], cc_2=[b2,b5], cod_1=[b3,b6]
    assert.Equal(t, []string{"b1", "b4"}, result["cc_1"])
    assert.Equal(t, []string{"b2", "b5"}, result["cc_2"])
    assert.Equal(t, []string{"b3", "b6"}, result["cod_1"])
}
```

### Edge Cases
- Empty agents list -> error
- Empty beads list -> empty result
- Single agent -> all beads to one agent
- More agents than beads -> some agents get nothing
- Uneven distribution -> remainder spread

### Logging Requirements
```go
func TestWithLogging(t *testing.T) {
    t.Logf("[TEST-UNIT-RR] test=%s input_agents=%d input_beads=%d",
        t.Name(), len(agents), len(beads))

    result := RoundRobin(agents, beads)

    for agent, assigned := range result {
        t.Logf("[TEST-UNIT-RR] agent=%s assigned_count=%d beads=%v",
            agent, len(assigned), assigned)
    }

    t.Logf("[TEST-UNIT-RR] test=%s status=complete total_assigned=%d",
        t.Name(), totalAssigned(result))
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Basic distribution works correctly
- [ ] All edge cases handled
- [ ] Deterministic ordering
- [ ] Verbose logging for debugging
- [ ] Tests run in < 1s","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:00.355116409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:22:33.354688322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:22:33.354161951Z","close_reason":"Tests already exist and pass all acceptance criteria. Found 12 comprehensive round-robin tests in internal/assign/matcher_test.go covering: even/uneven distribution, edge cases (empty inputs, single bead, more agents than beads), score/confidence=1.0, deterministic ordering, reason format, bead ordering preservation, and filtering unavailable agents. All tests pass in 0.004s.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1b9f","depends_on_id":"bd-l4y4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:22:31.779226585Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1b9f","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:40.100740064Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,266 @@
+package swarm
+
+import (
+	"path/filepath"
+	"sort"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/config"
+)
+
+// AllocationCalculator computes agent allocations for projects based on
+// bead counts and tier thresholds from SwarmConfig.
+type AllocationCalculator struct {
+	// Config holds the swarm configuration with tier thresholds and allocations.
+	Config *config.SwarmConfig
+}
+
+// NewAllocationCalculator creates a new AllocationCalculator with the given config.
+func NewAllocationCalculator(cfg *config.SwarmConfig) *AllocationCalculator {
+	return &AllocationCalculator{
+		Config: cfg,
+	}
+}
+
+// CalculateTier returns the tier number (1, 2, or 3) for a given bead count.
+func (ac *AllocationCalculator) CalculateTier(beadCount int) int {
+	if beadCount >= ac.Config.Tier1Threshold {
+		return 1
+	}
+	if beadCount >= ac.Config.Tier2Threshold {
+		return 2
+	}
+	return 3
+}
+
+// CalculateProjectAllocation computes the allocation for a single project.
+func (ac *AllocationCalculator) CalculateProjectAllocation(project ProjectBeadCount) ProjectAllocation {
+	tier := ac.CalculateTier(project.OpenBeads)
+	alloc := ac.Config.GetAllocationForBeadCount(project.OpenBeads)
+
+	// Update the project's tier if not already set
+	project.Tier = tier
+
+	return ProjectAllocation{
+		Project:     project,
+		CCAgents:    alloc.CC,
+		CodAgents:   alloc.Cod,
+		GmiAgents:   alloc.Gmi,
+		TotalAgents: alloc.Total(),
+	}
+}
+
+// CalculateAllocations computes allocations for all projects.
+// Projects are sorted by bead count descending (highest priority first).
+func (ac *AllocationCalculator) CalculateAllocations(projects []ProjectBeadCount) []ProjectAllocation {
+	if len(projects) == 0 {
+		return []ProjectAllocation{}
+	}
+
+	// Sort projects by bead count descending
+	sorted := make([]ProjectBeadCount, len(projects))
+	copy(sorted, projects)
+	sort.Slice(sorted, func(i, j int) bool {
+		return sorted[i].OpenBeads > sorted[j].OpenBeads
+	})
+
+	allocations := make([]ProjectAllocation, len(sorted))
+	for i, project := range sorted {
+		allocations[i] = ac.CalculateProjectAllocation(project)
+	}
+
+	return allocations
+}
+
+// AggregateTotals computes the total agents by type across all allocations.
+type AggregateTotals struct {
+	TotalCC     int
+	TotalCod    int
+	TotalGmi    int
+	TotalAgents int
+}
+
+// CalculateTotals sums up agent counts across all allocations.
+func (ac *AllocationCalculator) CalculateTotals(allocations []ProjectAllocation) AggregateTotals {
+	var totals AggregateTotals
+	for _, alloc := range allocations {
+		totals.TotalCC += alloc.CCAgents
+		totals.TotalCod += alloc.CodAgents
+		totals.TotalGmi += alloc.GmiAgents
+		totals.TotalAgents += alloc.TotalAgents
+	}
+	return totals
+}
+
+// ProjectBeadCountFromPath creates a ProjectBeadCount from a path and bead count.
+// The name is extracted from the last component of the path.
+func ProjectBeadCountFromPath(path string, openBeads int) ProjectBeadCount {
+	return ProjectBeadCount{
+		Path:      path,
+		Name:      filepath.Base(path),
+		OpenBeads: openBeads,
+	}
+}
+
+// AllocationResult contains the complete allocation calculation result.
+type AllocationResult struct {
+	Allocations []ProjectAllocation
+	Totals      AggregateTotals
+}
+
+// Calculate performs the complete allocation calculation for a set of projects.
+// Returns allocations sorted by priority (highest bead count first) and totals.
+func (ac *AllocationCalculator) Calculate(projects []ProjectBeadCount) AllocationResult {
+	allocations := ac.CalculateAllocations(projects)
+	totals := ac.CalculateTotals(allocations)
+
+	return AllocationResult{
+		Allocations: allocations,
+		Totals:      totals,
+	}
+}
+
+// GenerateSwarmPlan creates a complete SwarmPlan from projects.
+// This includes allocations, totals, and session specifications.
+func (ac *AllocationCalculator) GenerateSwarmPlan(scanDir string, projects []ProjectBeadCount) *SwarmPlan {
+	result := ac.Calculate(projects)
+
+	// Calculate panes per session
+	maxAgentsPerType := max(result.Totals.TotalCC, result.Totals.TotalCod, result.Totals.TotalGmi)
+	sessionsPerType := ac.Config.SessionsPerType
+	panesPerSession := 0
+	if sessionsPerType > 0 && maxAgentsPerType > 0 {
+		panesPerSession = (maxAgentsPerType + sessionsPerType - 1) / sessionsPerType // Ceiling division
+	}
+
+	// Generate session specifications
+	sessions := ac.generateSessions(result.Allocations, sessionsPerType, panesPerSession)
+
+	return &SwarmPlan{
+		CreatedAt:       time.Now().UTC(),
+		ScanDir:         scanDir,
+		Allocations:     result.Allocations,
+		TotalCC:         result.Totals.TotalCC,
+		TotalCod:        result.Totals.TotalCod,
+		TotalGmi:        result.Totals.TotalGmi,
+		TotalAgents:     result.Totals.TotalAgents,
+		SessionsPerType: sessionsPerType,
+		PanesPerSession: panesPerSession,
+		Sessions:        sessions,
+	}
+}
+
+// generateSessions creates session specifications for all agent types.
+func (ac *AllocationCalculator) generateSessions(allocations []ProjectAllocation, sessionsPerType, panesPerSession int) []SessionSpec {
+	var sessions []SessionSpec
+
+	// Generate CC sessions
+	ccSessions := ac.generateSessionsForType("cc", allocations, sessionsPerType, panesPerSession,
+		func(a ProjectAllocation) int { return a.CCAgents })
+	sessions = append(sessions, ccSessions...)
+
+	// Generate Codex sessions
+	codSessions := ac.generateSessionsForType("cod", allocations, sessionsPerType, panesPerSession,
+		func(a ProjectAllocation) int { return a.CodAgents })
+	sessions = append(sessions, codSessions...)
+
+	// Generate Gemini sessions
+	gmiSessions := ac.generateSessionsForType("gmi", allocations, sessionsPerType, panesPerSession,
+		func(a ProjectAllocation) int { return a.GmiAgents })
+	sessions = append(sessions, gmiSessions...)
+
+	return sessions
+}
+
+// generateSessionsForType creates sessions for a specific agent type.
+func (ac *AllocationCalculator) generateSessionsForType(
+	agentType string,
+	allocations []ProjectAllocation,
+	sessionsPerType int,
+	panesPerSession int,
+	getAgentCount func(ProjectAllocation) int,
+) []SessionSpec {
+	// Build a flat list of panes by iterating through projects
+	var panes []PaneSpec
+	globalAgentIndex := 0
+
+	for _, alloc := range allocations {
+		agentCount := getAgentCount(alloc)
+		for i := 0; i < agentCount; i++ {
+			globalAgentIndex++
+			panes = append(panes, PaneSpec{
+				Index:      globalAgentIndex,
+				Project:    alloc.Project.Path,
+				AgentType:  agentType,
+				AgentIndex: i + 1,
+				LaunchCmd:  agentType,
+			})
+		}
+	}
+
+	if len(panes) == 0 {
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	// Distribute panes across sessions
+	sessions := make([]SessionSpec, 0, sessionsPerType)
+	paneIdx := 0
+
+	for sessionNum := 1; sessionNum <= sessionsPerType && paneIdx < len(panes); sessionNum++ {
+		sessionName := generateSessionName(agentType, sessionNum)
+		sessionPanes := make([]PaneSpec, 0, panesPerSession)
+
+		// Fill this session with panes
+		for j := 0; j < panesPerSession && paneIdx < len(panes); j++ {
+			pane := panes[paneIdx]
+			pane.Index = j + 1 // 1-based index within session
+			sessionPanes = append(sessionPanes, pane)
+			paneIdx++
+		}
+
+		if len(sessionPanes) > 0 {
+			sessions = append(sessions, SessionSpec{
+				Name:      sessionName,
+				AgentType: agentType,
+				PaneCount: len(sessionPanes),
+				Panes:     sessionPanes,
+			})
+		}
+	}
+
+	return sessions
+}
+
+// generateSessionName creates a session name in the format "{type}_agents_{num}".
+func generateSessionName(agentType string, sessionNum int) string {
+	return agentType + "_agents_" + itoa(sessionNum)
+}
+
+// itoa converts an int to a string without importing strconv.
+func itoa(i int) string {
+	if i == 0 {
+		return "0"
+	}
+
+	neg := i < 0
+	if neg {
+		i = -i
+	}
+
+	// Max int64 is 19 digits
+	buf := make([]byte, 20)
+	pos := len(buf)
+
+	for i > 0 {
+		pos--
+		buf[pos] = byte('0' + i%10)
+		i /= 10
+	}
+
+	if neg {
+		pos--
+		buf[pos] = '-'
+	}
+
+	return string(buf[pos:])
+}
